checks.a_analysis: The JSON output contains headings and paragraphs that directly correspond to the source PDF. No fabricated text or invented section descriptions (e.g., 'Page 2 Content') were found.
The URL 'www.startwithyourheart.com' present in the output correctly matches the source document. No other contact information (phone numbers, addresses) exists in the source or output.

All major sections of the document, spanning all four pages, are represented in the JSON output, including 'What Is Blood Pressure?', 'How Is Blood Pressure Checked?', 'Risk Factors You Cannot Control', 'Take Control for Good Health', and 'Goals and Information'.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Most text content from the source sections is preserved. While some minor text fragments (labels and footer) are missing, these do not constitute a 'significant' omission (greater than 25%) of any section's content. The core instructional and informational text is intact.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: There are several minor text omissions throughout the document. The text labels for diagrams on Page 1 ('Heart', 'Arteries in the heart', 'Blood flowing through arteries') and Page 2 ('Systolic (top number)', 'Diastolic (bottom number)') are missing as independent text elements. On Page 4, the branding text 'Start With Your Heart' and organization name 'Justus-Warren Heart Disease & Stroke Prevention Task Force' are omitted. Finally, the printing information footer on Page 4 ('22,000 copies of this public document...') is also absent. While these do not impact the primary health information, they are omissions of text present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Heart; Arteries in the heart; Blood flowing through arteries; Systolic (top number); Diastolic (bottom number); Start With Your Heart; Justus-Warren Heart Disease & Stroke Prevention Task Force; 22,000 copies of this public document were printed at a cost of $0.13 per copy. 09/11.
checks.b_evidence_output: The listed text strings are omitted from the JSON output's text elements.

checks.a_analysis: The converted document follows the source PDF's structure page by page without duplicating any text blocks, paragraphs, or sections. Each piece of information, from the definition of blood pressure to the goal-setting items at the end, appears exactly once in the output, mirroring its appearance in the source.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The document uses an H2 for the main call to action and H3s for the specific question-based headings within the sections. Subsection titles correctly capture the major headers (red bars) from the source document, maintaining a clean nested structure (e.g., H2 subtitle followed by H3 questions).

The source document does not contain any tables. The 3x3 grid of risk reduction tips on page 3 and the goal-setting checkboxes on page 4 are correctly interpreted as lists rather than tables, which is appropriate for their content and structure.

The reading order follows the source document's flow perfectly, proceeding from page 1 to page 4. Content within each section is ordered logically, such as the explanation of blood pressure readings coming before the risk factors and health tips. The placement of images relative to the text is also preserved or logically adjusted for accessibility.

The document identifies three distinct list structures: risk factors (page 2), health tips (page 3), and goal-setting items (page 4). All are correctly captured as unordered lists with the appropriate number of items. The grid-based layout of the health tips in the source is successfully flattened into a continuous list without merging or losing any items.
The single external link to 'www.startwithyourheart.com' is correctly identified and linked to the accurate URL. No extraneous or fabricated links have been added to the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output exhibits a significant visual flow issue. In the source PDF, the photograph of a woman with a headache is visually and contextually tied to the heading 'What are the signs of high blood pressure?'. However, in the converted HTML, this image has been moved up into the 'What Is Blood Pressure?' section, appearing before the correct heading. Additionally, the illustrative diagrams (three circles) from the first section are missing, replaced by a generic green placeholder block, which disrupts the visual explanation provided in the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: On page 1 of the PDF, the image of a woman with her hands to her head is positioned directly next to/under the heading 'What are the signs of high blood pressure?'.
checks.b_evidence_output: In the HTML screenshot, the same image of the woman appears under the 'What Is Blood Pressure?' section, separated from the relevant 'signs' heading by a green block and the heading itself.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'What Is Blood Pressure?' and 'What are the signs of high blood pressure?'

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF does not contain any data tables. The layout elements are primarily text blocks, images, and lists.

A significant number of meaningful images are missing or failed to render in the HTML output. These include the three circular diagrams on page 1 explaining anatomy, the digital blood pressure monitor diagram on page 2 (which is critical for understanding systolic vs diastolic numbers), all nine illustrative photos for health tips on page 3, and the family photos and organization logo on page 4. Many of these are replaced by large green placeholder blocks or are simply absent.
checks.b_evidence_source: Meaningful images on Page 1 (3 circles: Heart, Arteries, Blood flow), Page 2 (Digital monitor diagram), Page 3 (9 tip photos), and Page 4 (family photos and 'Start With Your Heart' logo).
checks.b_evidence_output: The images are missing. The circular diagrams and digital monitor are replaced by green rectangular blocks; the 9 health tip images and page 4 photos are absent entirely.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document; specifically under 'What Is Blood Pressure?', 'How Is Blood Pressure Checked?', 'Take Control for Good Health', and 'Goals and Information'.

checks.a_analysis: No body content (paragraphs, lists, or sections) is visually duplicated in the rendered HTML. Section headings appearing in the Table of Contents and then again in the body is standard accessible design and does not count as duplication.
